repo_prior_art.cite: eid_data_fusion_k_source_v1: its overlap-hedge/source-cover proposal does not state label-restricted two-block context-marginal invariance.

published_prior_art.cite: LeeCorreaBareinboim2020General gives hedgelets/thickets for arbitrary supplied experiments, not selector-induced label restrictions that erase a shared coupling in every supplied context.

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_data_fusion_k_source_v1: related unfinished overlap-hedge converse, but no terminal SS-ID CS-hedge or full parity-pasting certificate.

published_prior_art.cite: LeeGhassamiShpitser2024Systematic proves nonidentification for covered failure lines and leaves one failure case open; no published CS-hedge certificate was verified.

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_data_fusion_k_source_v1: broad systematic-selection witness gap, not a characterization of the exact confounded-selector terminal trace.

published_prior_art.cite: LeeGhassamiShpitser2024Systematic, Theorems 4–5 and Algorithm 2: soundness and three covered failure lines are proved, while the remaining Algorithm-2 terminal failure is outside Theorem 5 and completeness is conjectured.
LeeGhassamiShpitser2024Systematic already supplies SS-ID soundness and covered nonidentification cases; conditional on Open-ended Question 1, the iff is their routine completeness closure.
ShpitserPearl2006Joint: ordinary rooted hedges witness nonidentification of classical interventional queries.

novelty_flags.label: related_work / thm:context-marginal-sufficiency
novelty_flags.one_line: ShpitserPearl2006Joint is bibliographed as the UAI conditional-effects paper (arXiv:1206.6876), which only reviews and cites the ordinary hedge result; the ordinary hedge and binary construction invoked here are from Shpitser and Pearl's separate AAAI 2006 joint-interventional-distributions paper.

soundness_flags.label: def:ordinary-hedge / def:cs-hedge / thm:context-marginal-sufficiency
soundness_flags.one_line: The declared ordinary-hedge class has no condition linking its roots to Y (such as R⊆An(Y) in the appropriately mutilated graph); hence a CS-hedge can live in a component disconnected from Y, while ParityPaste fixes the remainder, so Qε=Q−ε and the universal target-separation theorem is false under its own definitions.

soundness_flags.label: def:cs-model-class / ass:selector-state-encoding / ass:label-restriction
soundness_flags.one_line: The compatible-model definition permits arbitrary equations Xv=fv(pa,Uv,S), including for v=S, but does not impose the selector-child switch semantics required for an LS-ADMMG; therefore the claimed label deletion and rhoS-based pasting are not determined by a CS-SCM.

soundness_flags.label: def:cs-hedge / def:parity-pasting
soundness_flags.one_line: Connu is undefined, and XOR/binary response functions are applied although the declared state spaces are arbitrary finite sets; the certificate and Mκ are consequently not defined for the stated model class.

soundness_flags.label: estimand_functional / def:cs-fail-2-class
soundness_flags.one_line: Q=FSS-ID(...) is stated without a success qualifier even though FSS-ID is defined only on successful traces and every member of Ccs2 is an SS-ID failure instance.

soundness_flags.label: ass:selector-state-encoding
soundness_flags.one_line: LeeGhassamiShpitser2024Systematic defines S as child-specific intervention-indicator/value pairs, not an arbitrary powerset label encoding; retag this as a constructed encoding and give the explicit correspondence.

soundness_flags.label: ass:label-restriction
soundness_flags.one_line: The cited LS-ADMMG restriction deletes an edge when S is serious for a vertex in its derived label; the proposed arbitrary lambda(e)∩u rule is not the cited standard condition without a semantics-preserving label map.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: thm:ss-id-completeness-frontier / honest_scope
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Completeness is claimed only conditional on a terminal cs_fail_2 nonidentification certificate, and only for finite CS-SCM instances with positive supplied selector states.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: If the repaired Open-ended Question 1 is answered affirmatively, SS-ID is complete on the explicitly defined finite positive-support CS-SCM class.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Redefine the hedge relative to the target by adding the classical root-to-outcome ancestry condition and then prove the no-selection reduction with an explicit disconnected-Y counterexample ruled out.

Replace the generic powerset selector with an explicit encoding of each selector child’s indicator and intervention value; define the induced LS-ADMMG labels and prove that rho_u is exactly the published serious-context restriction.

Restrict the parity construction to explicitly binary witness vertices (or supply alphabet-preserving embeddings), define Connu and all equations including the selector equation, and exhibit a small labelled ADMMG where every supplied context law agrees but the target differs.

Split the successful-side identifying functional from the failed-side target definition, and correct the Shpitser–Pearl bibliography to the AAAI 2006 joint-interventional-distributions paper used by the parity argument.

recommended_next_step: Return to Stage -1 for a substantive rebuild of the CS-SCM/hedge semantics and target-ancestry condition, then rerun novelty review.
